body: WARRANT#85 shipped -> SLS(90)/10.04. Completed forge-indicate Pillar C's 'shrinkage-to-prior' for the relationship layer: RI_PairwiseLift had a hard 0.0 cliff below the diagnostic floor rather than the smooth empirical-Bayes shrinkage Living-Edge cells already get (LE_ShrunkMean, WARRANT#33). Added RI_PairwiseLiftShrunk as a genuinely new, parallel function -- deliberately did not modify RI_PairwiseLift itself, since another function (ProbDB_AnnotateDriverAgreement) explicitly depends on its exact 0.0 meaning 'unmeasured, skip' as a sentinel, and a smooth shrinkage would silently break that. Surfaced alongside the existing raw-lift figure in the relationship report. Diagnostic only. Compiled 0 errors, deployed, ledger at pass_70. Five warrants now this session (#80-85, six counting the diagnostic-only #81) -- this is a real, genuine floor: what's left in forge-indicate's Pillar C (bounded nonlinearity/interaction terms) correctly can't be built without live OOS data to validate against, and everything else flagged across every lane this session traces back to the same two blockers -- the Terminal-closing mystery (needs your hands-on investigation, not more code) and your own judgment calls (the champion-reversion trigger threshold, whether to flip any of the gated flags on). I don't think there's more legitimate code-only work left to find without either of those -- want me to keep digging for smaller things, or would it be more useful to look at the Terminal-closing issue next? Source, ex4, ledger attached.
